extra.reasoning:
The property requires that no input makes both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b true simultaneously.

rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50): IF (elderly AND g6pd_deficiency AND heart_failure). Otherwise it defaults to false.

rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50): IF elderly. Otherwise it defaults to false.

The condition that makes rec_drug_a true (elderly AND g6pd_deficiency AND heart_failure) is a strict subset of the condition that makes rec_drug_b true (elderly). Thus every input that fires the drug-A rule also fires the drug-B rule, since elderly is a required conjunct in both. Priorities and tie-breaking are irrelevant here because each output has only one positive-setting rule plus the default; there is no competing rule that could override either to false.

Concretely, with elderly=true, g6pd_deficiency=true, heart_failure=true: rec_a_from_conjunction fires (rec_drug_a=true) and rec_b_from_trigger fires (rec_drug_b=true). Both are jointly true, violating mutual exclusion. immunosuppressed only affects rec_drug_c (via rec_c_from_immunosuppressed) and is irrelevant to this conflict; it is set to false in the witness. The property is violated.
